Tests the behavior of Math.floor with Infinity in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the `Math.floor` function when applied to the value `Infinity`. The assertion verifies that `Math.floor(Infinity)` returns `Infinity`, which is consistent with the mathematical definition of the floor function for infinite values. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case for the `Math.floor` method in JavaScript. It is likely used in a testing context to ensure that the JavaScript engine or environment correctly implements this aspect of the ECMAScript specification.
